info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Development Goals Mass Assignment Process
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

get.summary: Get all mass assignment processes for development goals

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • Assignee; integer; A code that determines the employees in the hierarchy of the requester who need to be assigned the development goals included in the mass assignment process.
  • CreatedBy; string; Name of the person who created the mass assignment process for development goals.
  • CreationDate; string; Date the mass assignment process for development goals was created.
  • LastUpdateDate; string; Date the mass assignment process for development goals was last updated.
  • LastUpdatedBy; string; Name of the person who last updated the mass assignment process for development goals.
  • MassProcessName; string; Name of the mass assignment process for development goals.
  • MassRequestId; integer; Unique identifier for the mass assignment request for development goals.
  • RequestOnBehalfOfPersonId; integer; Unique identifier for the person requesting the mass assignment process for development goals.
  • RequestSubmittedByPersonId; integer; Unique identifier for the person submitting the mass assignment process for development goals.
  • RequestTypeCode; string; Request type of the mass assignment process for development goals.
  • RequesterName; string; Display name of the person requesting the mass assignment process for development goals.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds all mass assignment processes for development goals that match the primary key criteria specified.
    Finder Variables
    • MassRequestId; integer; Unique identifier for the mass assignment request for development goals.
  • filterTalentPoolGoals Finds all mass assignment processes for development goals based on the specified process name or requester; string; 's name.
    Finder Variables
    • MassRequestId; integer; Unique identifier for the mass assignment request for development goals.
  • findByAdvancedSearch Finds all mass assignment processes for development goals based on the specified process name or requester; string; 's name.
    Finder Variables
    • processName; string; Name of the mass assignment process for development goals.
    • requester; string; Name of the person requesting the mass assignment process for development goals.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

findByAdvancedSearchQuery.description: Finds mass assignment processes for development goals that match the specified parameters.

post.summary: Create a mass assignment process for development goals

parameters.name: Upsert-Mode
parameters.description: Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, the server performs an Upsert operation instead of a Create operation. During an Upsert operation, the server attempts to find an existing resource that matches the payload. If a match is found, the server updates the existing resource instead of creating a new one. If not found or false (default), the server performs a Create operation. Note that the Upsert operation isn't supported for date-effective REST resources.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

devIncludedWorkers.description: The devIncludedWorkers resource is a child of the devGoalsMassAssignProcesses resource and provides workers who need to be assigned the development goals that are part of the mass assignment process.

addRequesterInfo.description: Adds a requester and persons reporting to the requester who need to be assigned the development goals included in the mass assignment process.

devEligibilityProfiles.description: The devEligibilityProfiles resource is a child of the devGoalsMassAssignProcesses resource and provides all eligibility profiles that are added to the mass assignment process of development goals.

removeRequesterInfo.description: Removes requester and persons reporting to them from the mass assignment process of development goals.

devExcludedWorkers.description: The devExcludedWorkers resource is a child of the devGoalsMassAssignProcesses resource and provides all the workers who are excluded from the mass assignment process of development goals.

devGoals.description: The developmentGoals resource provides the development goals of a worker.

copy.description: Copies the specified mass assignment process for development goals including child objects such as included workers, excluded workers, eligibility profiles, and goals.

post.summary: Add a requester and their hierarchy
post.description: Adds a requester and persons reporting to the requester who need to be assigned the development goals included in the mass assignment process.

parameters.name: devGoalsMassAssignProcessUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Development Goals Mass Assignment Process resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Development Goals Mass Assignment Process. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Development Goals Mass Assignment Process collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Development Goals Mass Assignment Process to get the hash key.

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

offset.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

query.description: Query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection is queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions.

limit.description: Count of maximum records to be retrieved.

searchFields.description: Search fields for the mass assignment of development goals query. The search field can be the name of the mass assignment process.

filters.description: Filters for the mass assignment of development goals query. These filters can include attributes such as the name of the person requesting the mass assignment of development goals or the name of the mass assignment process.

sort.description: Sort criteria for the mass assignment processes of development goals returned by the query. The sort criteria can include the process name or the date the mass assignment process was submitted.

displayFields.description: Attributes included in the response of the mass assignment of development goals query. The attributes can be the name of the mass assignment process or the ID of the mass assignment process.

autocompleteQuery.description: Indicates whether the query is completed automatically. Valid values are Y and N. The default value is Y.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

result.description: Response returned for the mass assignment of development goals query.

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

BusinessTitle.title: Job
BusinessTitle.description: Business title of the worker included in the mass assignment process of development goals.

CascadingLevel.title: Assignees
CascadingLevel.description: Code that determines which employees reporting to the requester will be assigned the development goals during the mass assignment process. The development goals can be assigned only to the requester, all direct reports of the requester, all direct reports of the requester and the requester, all reports of the requester, and all reports of the requesters and the requester.

CreatedBy.description: User who added the worker to the mass assignment process of development goals.

CreationDate.description: Date the worker was included in the mass assignment process of development goals.

DepartmentName.title: Department
DepartmentName.description: Name of the department that the worker included in the mass assignment process of development goals belongs to.

DirectsCount.description: Count of persons who report directly to the manager included in the mass assignment process of development goals.

DisplayName.description: Display name of the worker included in the mass assignment process of development goals.

LastUpdateDate.description: Date the details of the worker included in the mass assignment process of development goals was last updated.

devEligibilityProfiles.title: Development Eligibility Profiles
devEligibilityProfiles.description: The devEligibilityProfiles resource is a child of the devGoalsMassAssignProcesses resource and provides all eligibility profiles that are added to the mass assignment process of development goals.

devExcludedWorkers.title: Development Excluded Workers
devExcludedWorkers.description: The devExcludedWorkers resource is a child of the devGoalsMassAssignProcesses resource and provides all the workers who are excluded from the mass assignment process of development goals.

devGoals.title: Development Goals
devGoals.description: The developmentGoals resource provides the development goals of a worker.

devIncludedWorkers.title: Development Included Workers
devIncludedWorkers.description: The devIncludedWorkers resource is a child of the devGoalsMassAssignProcesses resource and provides workers who need to be assigned the development goals that are part of the mass assignment process.

Metadata-Context.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

REST-Framework-Version.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.
